modified: engine/rpc/server.go (FileId==0 InvalidArgument guard on GetFile/ReadPartial, non-blocking follow-up)

new_exported_symbols: rpc.RPCMetric struct { Method string; Duration time.Duration; RequestBytes int; ResponseBytes int; Code codes.Code }
unchanged_contracts: rpc.Server's 5 handler signatures (PutSegment/GetFile/ReadPartial/GraphNeighbors/SearchCandidates) unchanged in behavior; GetFile/ReadPartial gain an additional early-return InvalidArgument path for FileId==0 only, no change to any existing success/NotFound/Internal path

dependents: No current production caller of engine/rpc.Server over a real grpc.Server exists yet in this repo (no cmd/ binary) — interceptor is additive/unused-in-production until a future server binary or 3.2.5's integration test wires grpc.NewServer(grpc.UnaryInterceptor(rpc.LatencyInterceptor())) explicitly.
engine/split/proposer_grpc.go (gRPC client) unaffected — this is a server-side interceptor only.
engine/rpc/server_test.go's existing 15 subtests unaffected except the two new FileId=0 subtests appended.
risk_rationale: Purely additive observability layer plus a narrowly-scoped 2-line guard-clause fix in already-tested handlers; no change to existing success-path semantics, no new external dependencies, no proto/wire-format changes. Concurrency risk from the interceptor's own state (if any counters/aggregation added) is the only elevated-attention item; addressed by keeping the interceptor itself stateless per-call (delegates aggregation entirely to the pluggable Recorder, whose default implementation - log/slog - is already documented safe for concurrent use).
